title: Brittney Spencer found Bob Dylan in her dressing room. Here’s what happened next.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Brittney Spencer to open for Bob Dylan in Boston on July 16 at Leader Bank Pavilion.
Spencer met Bob Dylan in her dressing room two years ago after he saw her perform.
Spencer is known for collaborations with Willie Nelson, Beyoncé, and Grace Potter among others.

title: 5 of the best hotel restaurants in America are in New England, according to OpenTable
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Five New England hotel restaurants are among OpenTable's 2026 top 100 in America.
Massachusetts' Contessa at The Newbury Boston and Mooo Beacon Hill are featured in the list.
Maine's Earth at Hidden Pond and White Barn Inn, and Connecticut's Michael Jordan's Steak House are also included.

title: Revolution’s Marko Mitrović reflected on World Cup memories
author: Sam Lattof
bullets: Marko Mitrović is in his first season as head coach of the New England Revolution.
As a child in Belgrade, Mitrović developed a passion for soccer by watching World Cup tournaments.
Mitrović served as an assistant head coach for Serbia's U20 World Cup-winning team in 2015.

title: Joe Mazzulla showcased MMA training regimen, spoke about its influence on coaching the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla utilizes MMA training to enhance his coaching strategy, working with MMA trainer Eric Nicksick.
Mazzulla highlights the fighter's mindset and in-fight communication as influences on his coaching approach for the Celtics.
Mazzulla plans to continue MMA training to maintain a mental and physical edge in coaching the Celtics.

title: Report: Wrong-way driver who killed trooper had 9 drinks before Route 1 crash
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Driver Hernan Ramon Marrero was found to have a blood alcohol level of 0.192 after consuming nine drinks.
Marrero's wrong-way driving on Route 1 resulted in a collision that killed him and Trooper Kevin Trainor.
Trooper Trainor was praised for his courage in attempting to prevent the dangerous situation from worsening.

title: The best state to move to in 2026 is in New England — and it’s not Massachusetts
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: New Hampshire ranked as the best state to move to in 2026 by a ConsumerAffairs report.
Maine ranked fifth due to low crime rates and high air quality, while Massachusetts ranked sixth.
Boston's population decreased by 5,644 since 2020, driven by high housing costs and reduced immigration.

title: Acclaimed Seaport restaurant Chickadee to close after 8 years
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Chickadee, a James Beard-nominated restaurant in Boston's Seaport, will close permanently on July 18 after eight years.
Chef John DaSilva and beverage director Ted Kilpatrick opened Chickadee at 21 Drydock Ave. in 2018.
Chickadee was renowned for its Mediterranean-inspired dishes and earned national recognition, including several James Beard nominations.

title: Man charged with boating under the influence after Mass. college student drowns in Connecticut
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Michael Collins charged with boating under the influence after a fatal canoe accident on Lake Hayward, East Haddam.
Dominick Tocci, a 20-year-old Holy Cross student, drowned following the canoe capsizing; his body was found after two days.
Collins, also 20, turned himself in and was released on a court-issued promise to appear on July 23.

title: Nine years after South End killing, fugitive wanted on murder charge captured in North Carolina
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Fugitive Alan Marcus Lewis was arrested in Charlotte, North Carolina, for a 2017 murder in Boston's South End.
Lewis, charged with the murder of Brent Stevenson, evaded capture for nine years by living under aliases in Texas.
U.S. Marshals captured Lewis on July 1, and he will be extradited to Massachusetts to face the murder charge.

title: The people keeping Cape Cod’s car culture alive
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cape Cod Classics hosts over 20 annual events with nearly 500 members to support car enthusiasts and charities.
The Cape Cod British Car Club, with about 200 members, specializes in classic British cars and organizes local events.
Heritage Museums & Gardens in Sandwich restored 32 of 43 antique vehicles to working condition with volunteer efforts.
